howItWorks.intro: This pallet calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Boxes per layer = floor(Pallet L ÷ Box L) × floor(Pallet W ÷ Box W)
Also check rotated orientation and use whichever fits more
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: What is a standard pallet size?
faq.answer: The most common pallet in North America is 48×40 inches (GMA pallet). Euro pallets are 47.2×31.5 inches. Retail and grocery typically require GMA pallets.

faq.question: What is the maximum pallet height for shipping?
faq.answer: Standard max height is 48-60 inches including the pallet (5-6 inches). Most LTL carriers limit total height to 96 inches. Warehouses typically limit stack height to 48-60 inches of product.

faq.question: How much weight can a standard pallet hold?
faq.answer: A standard GMA pallet holds 2,500-3,000 lbs in static loading. Dynamic (forklift) capacity is about 2,500 lbs. However, LTL carriers often limit pallet weight to 2,000-2,500 lbs for handling safety.
